You are surveying candidate "audit pilot pairs" in the repo at /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster. A pilot pair = one AsciiDoc spec subdocument plus its implementing bash module(s), which will be audited together under the Allocation Coding Guide (ACG). The audit looks for: (a) "conceptual comments" in bash source — comments carrying design-time knowledge (what a thing IS, why it's shaped so, how it relates to other concepts) rather than operational mechanics; (b) "ornament" in the spec — sentences in normative position that neither constrain an implementer nor are marked as rationale; (c) paraphrase — the same normative idea worded differently in spec vs source comments.

Candidate area: AUTH / CREDENTIALS / ACCESS PROBES.
- Specs: Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRA-CredentialFormat.adoc, RBSAJ-access_jwt_probe.adoc, RBSAO-access_oauth_probe.adoc, RBSAX-access_setup.adoc, and glance at RBSRO-RegimeOauth.adoc
- Sources: Tools/rbk/rba_Auth.sh, Tools/rbk/rbgo_OAuth.sh, Tools/rbk/rbgv_AccessProbe.sh

Report, with search breadth medium:
1. Pair coherence: which spec(s) map onto which source file? Propose the cleanest pairing(s). Note: rbgo_OAuth.sh is known to contain load-bearing retry loops and a JWT-bearer mint flow — comments explaining WHY (token semantics, transient-error classification) are prime audit specimens.
2. Audit richness: quote 3-5 short specimens of design-time/conceptual comments in the source files (file:line + comment text, truncated). Quote 2-3 specimens of spec prose that looks like unmarked ornament or restates something the source also states.
3. Sizes: line counts of the specs and source files.
4. Churn: run `git log --oneline --since="2026-05-01" -- <the files>` and report commit counts, and whether messages carry heat tags ₣BH or ₣BZ (₣BZ is a heat actively reshaping account-role/citizen vocabulary — Governor/Payor/Manor/Depot terms; if these specs lean on that vocabulary, flag it).
5. Verdict line per candidate pairing: rich and quiet, or thin, or hot/contested?

Return a compact structured report. Do not modify anything.

- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

4	This regime specifies variables that define service account authentication credentials within the Recipe Bottle system.
5	Each
6	{rbra_regime}
7	instance contains the minimum identity and signing material needed for a specific service account role to authenticate to GCP APIs.
8	RBRA assignment files are external credential files stored outside the repository, in the directory specified by
9	{rbrr_secrets_dir}.
10	The paths
11	{rbrr_governor_rbra_file},

13	and

17	//axhrv_variable
18	{rbra_client_email}
19	
20	The service account email address assigned by GCP, used as the issuer (iss) when constructing JWTs for OAuth2 token exchange.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_access_jwt_probe}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation probes
5	{gar_service}
6	read access for a specified
7	{rbtr_role}
8	by performing a JWT-to-OAuth token exchange and then calling the Artifact Registry packages.list API.
9	It is a bounded propagation-absorbing retry, not a sustained-access check: the first successful response wins and the operation returns immediately.
10	A freshly-granted IAM role is not yet readable during the post-grant propagation window, surfacing as HTTP 403; the operation re-attempts — re-exchanging the token each time — until access propagates or the attempt budget is exhausted.
11	The operation's own defaults are a single attempt with no delay (`--count` 1, `--delay_ms` 0); the SA-role propagation budget (e.g. 60 attempts at 3000ms spacing) is supplied by the dispatching call-site, not baked into this operation.
12	
13	The operation accepts a role parameter selecting among
14	{rbtr_governor},
15	{rbtr_director},
16	and
17	{rbtr_retriever},
18	each with Owner, repoAdmin, and reader permissions respectively.
19	All three roles have Artifact Registry read access, so a successful response confirms both token validity and role-specific permission.
20	
21	//axhos_step
22	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
23	.. {rbbc_require}
24	`«INPUT_ROLE»` provided as first argument: one of `governor`, `director`, or `retriever`
25	.. {rbbc_fatal}
26	if role missing: "Role parameter required (governor|director|retriever)"
27	.. {rbbc_fatal}
28	if role unrecognized: "Unknown role: «INPUT_ROLE» — must be governor, director, or retriever"
29	.. {rbbc_store}
30	`«MAX_ATTEMPTS»` from `--count` flag (default: 1) — the maximum number of probe attempts, not a fixed iteration count; the first successful response ends the loop early
31	.. {rbbc_store}
32	`«DELAY_MS»` from `--delay_ms` flag (default: 0) — milliseconds to sleep between retries
33	.. {rbbc_fatal}
34	if `«MAX_ATTEMPTS»` is not a positive integer: "Count must be a positive integer"
35	.. {rbbc_fatal}
36	if `«DELAY_MS»` is not a non-negative integer: "Delay must be a non-negative integer"

73	
74	//axhos_step
75	. *Execute Bounded Probe Attempts*:
76	.. For each attempt `«ATTEMPT»` from 1 to `«MAX_ATTEMPTS»` (the loop ends early on the first successful response):
77	
78	... *Acquire «ROLE_DISPLAY» Token*:
79	.... Execute
80	{rbtoe_jwt_oauth_exchange}

82	.... {rbbc_store}

84	.... {rbbc_fatal}

90	..... method: `GET`

92	..... header: `Authorization: Bearer «ROLE_TOKEN»`
93	.... The call absorbs transient server-side errors internally: on HTTP 500, 502, 503, or 504 it re-executes up to 3 total attempts with exponential backoff (2s, then 4s), and {rbbc_fatal} if all 3 return a 5xx status: "«ROLE_DISPLAY» repeated transient HTTP «HTTP_STATUS» after 3 attempts". 107	..... If `«ATTEMPT»` is less than `«MAX_ATTEMPTS»`:
108	...... {rbbc_show}

111	..... Otherwise (the final attempt is still 403):
112	...... {rbbc_fatal}

4	This regime specifies OAuth 2.0 credentials used by the Payor role for token generation and API authentication.
5	RBRO assignment files are external credential files stored in ~/.rbw/payor/rbro.env with 600 permissions, never committed to version control.
6	
7	//axhrv_variable
8	{rbro_client_secret}
9	
10	OAuth 2.0 client secret from the Google Cloud Console OAuth client configuration.
11	Required; string validated.
12	Security-sensitive: masked in render output, never logged.
13	
14	//axhrv_variable
15	{rbro_refresh_token}
16	
17	Long-lived OAuth refresh token obtained during the authorization flow.
18	Used to obtain fresh access tokens for Payor operations.
19	Required; string validated.
20	Security-sensitive: masked in render output, never logged.
21	
22	//axhro_kindle
23	{rbkro_kindle}
24	
25	Sets defaults for both RBRO variables.
26	Detects unexpected RBRO_ variables via compgen.
27	Builds ZRBRO_ROLLUP with both values masked.
28	
29	//axhro_validate
30	{rbkro_validate}
31	
32	Validates RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET as non-empty string.
33	Validates RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN as non-empty string.
34	Dies on unexpected RBRO_ variables.
35	
36	//axhro_render
37	{rbkro_render}
38	
39	Renders via rbcr_* shared module.
40	Section: OAuth Credentials.
41	Both RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET and RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN displayed as masked (redacted with character count).
42	Validates after full render completes.

43	######################################################################

46	rba_get_governor_token_capture() {

59	rba_authenticate_role_capture() {
60	  zrba_sentinel
61	
62	  local -r z_rbra_file="${1}"
63	
64	  test -n "${z_rbra_file}" || return 1
65	  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || return 1
66	
67	  buc_log_args "Authenticating with RBRA file: ${z_rbra_file}"

79	rba_extract_json_to_rbra() {
80	  zrba_sentinel
81	
82	  local -r z_json_path="$1"
83	  local -r z_rbra_path="$2"
84	  local -r z_lifetime_sec="$3"
85	  local -r z_expected_project_id="${4:-}"
86	
87	  test -f "${z_json_path}" || buc_die "Service account JSON not found: ${z_json_path}"
88	
89	  buc_info "Extracting service account credentials from JSON"
90	
91	  buc_log_args 'Extract fields'
92	  local z_client_email
93	  z_client_email=$(jq -r '.client_email' "${z_json_path}") \
94	                                        || buc_die "Failed to extract client_email"
95	  test -n "${z_client_email}"           || buc_die "Empty client_email in JSON"
96	  test    "${z_client_email}" != "null" || buc_die "Null client_email in JSON"
97	
98	  local z_private_key
99	  z_private_key=$(jq -r '.private_key' "${z_json_path}") \
100	                                       || buc_die "Failed to extract private_key"
101	  test -n "${z_private_key}"           || buc_die "Empty private_key in JSON"
102	  test    "${z_private_key}" != "null" || buc_die "Null private_key in JSON"
103	
104	  local z_project_id
105	  z_project_id=$(jq -r '.project_id' "${z_json_path}") \
106	                                      || buc_die "Failed to extract project_id"
107	  test -n "${z_project_id}"           || buc_die "Empty project_id in JSON"
108	  test    "${z_project_id}" != "null" || buc_die "Null project_id in JSON"
109	
110	  if test -n "${z_expected_project_id}"; then
111	    buc_log_args "Verify project matches expected: ${z_expected_project_id}"
112	    test "${z_project_id}" = "${z_expected_project_id}" \
113	      || buc_die "Project mismatch: JSON has '${z_project_id}', expected '${z_expected_project_id}'"
114	  else
115	    buc_log_args "No project validation - accepting JSON project_id: ${z_project_id}"
116	  fi
117	
118	  buc_log_args 'Write RBRA file'
119	  # CAUTION: jq -r unescapes JSON \n to real newlines, so z_private_key holds a
120	  # multi-line PEM string. printf '%s' below preserves real newlines into the
121	  # RBRA file. Consumer (rbgo_OAuth.sh:zrbgo_build_jwt_capture) tolerates either
122	  # real-newline or '\n'-escape form via printf '%b'; do not "normalize" to one
123	  # form without auditing the consumer.
124	  {
125	    printf 'RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL="%s"\n'      "${z_client_email}"
126	    printf 'RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY="'; printf '%s' "${z_private_key}"; printf '"\n'
127	    printf 'RBRA_PROJECT_ID="%s"\n'        "${z_project_id}"
128	    printf 'RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC=%s\n'  "${z_lifetime_sec}"
129	  } > "${z_rbra_path}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_path}"
130	
131	  test -f "${z_rbra_path}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file: ${z_rbra_path}"
132	
133	  buc_warn "Consider deleting source JSON after verification: ${z_json_path}"
134	}

136	# RBTOE: RBRA Load Pattern
137	# Sources an RBRA file and validates required fields
138	rba_rbra_load() {
139	  zrba_sentinel
140	
141	  local -r z_rbra_file="${1}"
142	
143	  test -n "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "rba_rbra_load: RBRA file path required"
144	  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "rba_rbra_load: RBRA file not found: ${z_rbra_file}"
145	
146	  buc_log_args "Loading and validating RBRA credentials from ${z_rbra_file}"
147	
148	  # Source the RBRA file
149	  source "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "rba_rbra_load: failed to source RBRA file"
150	
151	  # Validate required fields
152	  test -n "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}" || buc_die "rba_rbra_load: RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL missing from ${z_rbra_file}"
153	  test -n "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY:-}" || buc_die "rba_rbra_load: RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY missing from ${z_rbra_file}"
154	  test -n "${RBRA_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "rba_rbra_load: RBRA_PROJECT_ID missing from ${z_rbra_file}"
155	
156	  # Check for null values
157	  test "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL}" != "null" || buc_die "rba_rbra_load: RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL is null in ${z_rbra_file}"
158	  test "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY}" != "null" || buc_die "rba_rbra_load: RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY is null in ${z_rbra_file}"
159	  test "${RBRA_PROJECT_ID}" != "null" || buc_die "rba_rbra_load: RBRA_PROJECT_ID is null in ${z_rbra_file}"
160	
161	  buc_log_args "RBRA validation successful: ${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL} in project ${RBRA_PROJECT_ID}"
162	}
163	
164	# RBTOE: RBRO Load Pattern
165	# Thin wrapper: defensively sources rbro_regime.sh (callers don't need to know
166	# its path, which moved under AAD's payor/ subdirectory migration), then
167	# delegates to rbro_load. Parallels rba_rbra_load at the call-signature level
168	# even though that function carries its own validation; the uniform rba_*
169	# load-through-utility convention is the load-bearing reason this wrapper exists.
170	rba_rbro_load() {
171	  zrba_sentinel
172	
173	  buc_log_args "Loading RBRO OAuth credentials"
174	
175	  # Source regime module if not already loaded
176	  if test -z "${ZRBRO_SOURCED:-}"; then
177	    source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbro_regime.sh"
178	  fi
179	
180	  # Delegate to regime's canonical load
181	  rbro_load
182	
183	  buc_log_args "RBRO validation successful"
184	}

2	#

16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Google OAuth - Service account JWT authentication
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBGO_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgo multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBGO_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbgo_*)
29	
30	zrbgo_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBGO_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgo already kindled"
32	
33	  # Validate required tools (rehomed from rbl_Locator.sh)
34	  command -v openssl >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "openssl not found - required for JWT signing and encoding"
35	  command -v curl    >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "curl not found - required for OAuth exchange"
36	  command -v jq      >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "jq not found - required for JSON parsing"
37	
38	  buc_log_args "Ensure RBGC is kindled first"
39	  zrbgc_sentinel
40	
41	  buc_log_args "Check environment"
42	  zburd_sentinel
43	
44	  buc_log_args "Set Module Variables (ZRBGO_*)"
45	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_header.json"
46	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_claims.json"
47	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_unsigned.txt"
48	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_signature.txt"
49	  readonly ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_oauth_response.json"
50	  readonly ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_openssl_stderr.txt"
51	  readonly ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_curl_stderr.txt"
52	  readonly ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jq_stderr.txt"
53	  readonly ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_consumer_retry_body.json"
54	
55	  readonly ZRBGO_KINDLED=1
56	}
57	
58	zrbgo_sentinel() {
59	  test "${ZRBGO_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgo not kindled - call zrbgo_kindle first"
60	}
61	
62	######################################################################
63	# Base64 primitives — generic openssl wrappers.
64	#
65	# Stateless — no sentinel; safe to call from any module regardless of
66	# kindle order. rbgo base64url-encodes JWT material; rbgg/rbgp decode SA
67	# private keys and rbfd decodes Cloud Build step outputs. The -A flag
68	# (suppress line wrapping) is load-bearing on every site and lives only
69	# here, so it cannot be silently dropped on one path.
70	
71	rbgo_base64_decode_string_to_file() {
72	  local -r z_b64="${1:-}"
73	  local -r z_output="${2:-}"
74	  test -n "${z_b64}"    || return 1
75	  test -n "${z_output}" || return 1
76	  printf '%s' "${z_b64}" | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > "${z_output}" || return 1
77	}
78	
79	rbgo_base64_decode_file_to_file() {
80	  local -r z_input="${1:-}"
81	  local -r z_output="${2:-}"
82	  test -n "${z_input}"  || return 1
83	  test -n "${z_output}" || return 1
84	  test -f "${z_input}"  || return 1
85	  openssl enc -base64 -d -A < "${z_input}" > "${z_output}" || return 1
86	}
87	
88	rbgo_base64_encode_string_capture() {
89	  local -r z_input="${1:-}"
90	  printf '%s' "${z_input}" | openssl enc -base64 -A
91	}
92	
93	rbgo_base64_encode_file_capture() {
94	  local -r z_file="${1:-}"
95	  test -f "${z_file}" || return 1
96	  openssl enc -base64 -A < "${z_file}"
97	}
98	
99	# Stateless — no sentinel; safe to call from any module regardless of kindle order.
100	rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate() {
101	  case "${1:-}" in
102	    7|28|35|56) return 0 ;;
103	    *)          return 1 ;;
104	  esac
105	}
106	
107	zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture() {
108	  zrbgo_sentinel
109	
110	  local -r z_input="${1:-}"
111	
112	  # Base64url: encode, then URL-safe transform and strip = padding
113	  local z_encoded
114	  z_encoded=$(rbgo_base64_encode_string_capture "${z_input}") || {
115	    buc_warn "openssl base64url encode failed"
116	    return 1
117	  }
118	  z_encoded="${z_encoded//+/-}"
119	  z_encoded="${z_encoded//\//_}"
120	  z_encoded="${z_encoded//=/}"
121	  printf '%s' "${z_encoded}"
122	}
123	
124	zrbgo_build_jwt_capture() {
125	  zrbgo_sentinel
126	
127	  local -r z_rbra_file="$1"
128	
129	  # RBRA_* expected: CLIENT_EMAIL, PRIVATE_KEY, TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC
130	  # RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY is sourced as a multi-line string (real newlines from PEM).
131	  # printf '%b' below is defensive and tolerates either real-newline or '\n'-escape
132	  # form; PEM keys contain no backslashes so %b is otherwise a no-op.
133	  # Only source if not already loaded (avoids readonly conflict in subshells)
134	  if test -z "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}"; then
135	    buc_log_args "Source RBRA file"
136	    source "${z_rbra_file}" || return 1
137	  fi
138	
139	  buc_log_args "Validate required variables"
140	  test -n "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}"       || return 1
141	  test -n "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"        || return 1
142	  test -n "${RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC:-}" || return 1
143	
144	  buc_log_args "Build JWT header"
145	  printf '%s' '{"alg":"RS256","typ":"JWT"}' > "${ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE}"
146	
147	  buc_log_args "Calculate timestamps"
148	  local z_now
149	  z_now=$(date +%s) || return 1
150	  local -r z_exp=$((z_now + RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC))
151	
152	  buc_log_args "Build JWT claims"
153	  jq -n                                         \
154	    --arg iss   "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL}"          \
155	    --arg scope "${RBGC_SCOPE_CLOUD_PLATFORM}"  \
156	    --arg aud   "${RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL}"       \
157	    --argjson iat "${z_now}"                    \
158	    --argjson exp "${z_exp}"                    \
159	    '{iss: $iss, scope: $scope, aud: $aud, iat: $iat, exp: $exp}' \
160	    > "${ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE}" || return 1
161	
162	  buc_log_args "Encode header and claims"
163	  local z_header_enc
164	  z_header_enc=$(zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture "$(<"${ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE}")") || return 1
165	
166	  local z_claims_enc
167	  z_claims_enc=$(zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture "$(<"${ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE}")") || return 1
168	
169	  buc_log_args "Create unsigned JWT"
170	  printf '%s' "${z_header_enc}.${z_claims_enc}" > "${ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE}"
171	
172	  buc_log_args "Sign with RSA256 (without writing key to disk)"
173	  # Convert literal \n sequences to real newlines via printf %b and feed via process substitution
174	  openssl dgst -sha256 \
175	    -sign <(printf '%b' "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY}\n") \
176	    -out "${ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE}" \
177	    "${ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE}" || {
178	      buc_warn "openssl RSA256 sign failed: $(<"${ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE}")"
179	      return 1
180	    }
181	
182	  buc_log_args "Base64url encode signature"
183	  local z_signature
184	  z_signature=$(rbgo_base64_encode_file_capture "${ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE}") || {
185	    buc_warn "openssl signature base64 encode failed"
186	    return 1
187	  }
188	  z_signature="${z_signature//+/-}"
189	  z_signature="${z_signature//\//_}"
190	  z_signature="${z_signature//=/}"
191	
192	  buc_log_args "Return complete JWT"
193	  printf '%s\n' "${z_header_enc}.${z_claims_enc}.${z_signature}"
194	}
195	
196	zrbgo_exchange_jwt_capture() {
197	  zrbgo_sentinel
198	
199	  local -r z_jwt="$1"
200	
201	  local z_attempt=0
202	  local z_curl_status=0
203	  while :; do
204	    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
205	
206	    buc_log_args "Exchange JWT for OAuth token (attempt ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS})"
207	    z_curl_status=0
208	    curl -sS -X POST "${RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL}"                                        \
209	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}"                           \
210	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"                                         \
211	      -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded"                           \
212	      -d "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer&assertion=${z_jwt}" \
213	      > "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE}" || z_curl_status=$?
214	
215	    test "${z_curl_status}" -eq 0 && break
216	
217	    buc_warn "OAuth curl exit ${z_curl_status}: $(<"${ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE}")"
218	    rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate "${z_curl_status}" || return 1
219	    test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}" || return 1
220	
221	    buc_step "OAuth token mint: transient curl ${z_curl_status}, retry ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} in ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}s"
222	    sleep "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}"
223	  done
224	
225	  # Scrubber filters by field NAME, not value. Deliberate best-effort log hygiene:
226	  # explicitly deletes access_token/refresh_token, then drops any field whose key
227	  # matches token|secret|key|password (case-insensitive) — catches id_token,
228	  # client_secret, etc. If the OAuth provider ever returns a new secret-carrying
229	  # field whose name doesn't match this regex, the scrub would miss it; update
230	  # the regex here when that happens.
231	  buc_log_args "Debug: Show the actual response (minus secrets)"
232	  jq 'del(.access_token, .refresh_token)
233	      | with_entries(select(.key | test("token|secret|key|password"; "i") | not))' \
234	    "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}" | buc_log_pipe || buc_log_args "OAuth response parsing failed"
235	
236	  buc_log_args "OAuth token exchange completed"
237	
238	  buc_log_args "Extract access token"
239	  local z_token
240	  z_token=$(jq -r '.access_token // empty' "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}") || return 1
241	  test -n "${z_token}" || {
242	    buc_warn "OAuth response missing access_token; redacted response body follows:"
243	    jq 'with_entries(select(.key | test("token|secret|key|password"; "i") | not))' \
244	      "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}" >&2 \
245	      || printf '%s\n' "$(<"${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}")" >&2
246	    return 1
247	  }
248	
249	  printf '%s\n' "${z_token}"
250	}
251	
252	######################################################################
253	# External Functions (rbgo_*)
254	
255	rbgo_get_token_capture() {
256	  zrbgo_sentinel
257	
258	  local -r z_rbra_file="$1"
259	
260	  buc_doc_brief "Exchange service account credentials for OAuth2 access token (retries on JWT-propagation race)"
261	  buc_doc_param "rbra_file" "Path to RBRA credentials file containing RBRA_* variables"
262	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
263	
264	  buc_log_args "Validate RBRA file exists"
265	  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || return 1
266	
267	  local z_attempt=0
268	  local z_elapsed=0
269	  local z_delay="${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}"
270	  local z_jwt=""
271	  local z_token=""
272	  local z_status=0
273	  local z_err=""
274	  local z_err_desc=""
275	
276	  while :; do
277	    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
278	
279	    z_status=0
280	    z_jwt=$(zrbgo_build_jwt_capture "${z_rbra_file}") || z_status=$?
281	    test "${z_status}" -eq 0 || return 1
282	
283	    z_status=0
284	    z_token=$(zrbgo_exchange_jwt_capture "${z_jwt}") || z_status=$?
285	
286	    if test "${z_status}" -eq 0 && test -n "${z_token}"; then
287	      printf '%s\n' "${z_token}"
288	      return 0
289	    fi
290	
291	    # TEMP-FORENSIC ₢BBABL: capture every token-endpoint mint failure to a
292	    # stable, append-only absolute log so a later successful mint cannot clobber
293	    # the failing body (the memo's Host-B capture gap, memo-20260527). Purpose:
294	    # confirm the ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID token-endpoint shape before writing the
295	    # touchpoint-2 match. REMOVE once the shape is captured and the match lands.
296	    { printf '=== rbgo mint fail rbra=%s status=%s ===\n' "${z_rbra_file}" "${z_status}"
297	      printf '%s' "$(<"${ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE}")" "$(<"${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}")"
298	      printf '\n=== end ===\n'
299	    } >> /tmp/rbgo_BBABL_forensic.log 2>/dev/null || true
300	
301	    # Discriminate failure: only the SA-propagation race shapes retry —
302	    #   `invalid_grant` + `Invalid JWT Signature.`         (fresh-key propagation lag)
303	    #   `invalid_grant` + `Invalid grant: account not found` (fresh-SA propagation lag)
304	    # Any other shape fails fast.
305	    z_err=$(jq -r '.error // ""'             "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}") || z_err=""
306	    z_err_desc=$(jq -r '.error_description // ""' "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}") || z_err_desc=""
307	
308	    test "${z_err}" = "invalid_grant" || return 1
309	    case "${z_err_desc}" in
310	      "Invalid JWT Signature."|"Invalid grant: account not found") ;;
311	      *) return 1 ;;
312	    esac
313	
314	    cp "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" "${ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE}" || return 1
315	
316	    if test "${z_elapsed}" -ge "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_BUDGET_SEC}"; then
317	      buc_warn "Token mint: SA propagation race timeout after ${z_elapsed}s (${z_attempt} attempts); last body: ${ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE}"
318	      return 1
319	    fi
320	
321	    buc_step "Token mint: attempt ${z_attempt} hit SA propagation race (${z_err_desc}) at ${z_elapsed}s, sleep ${z_delay}s"
322	    sleep "${z_delay}"
323	    z_elapsed=$((z_elapsed + z_delay))
324	    z_delay=$((z_delay * 2))
325	    test "${z_delay}" -le "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_delay="${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC}"
326	  done
327	}

329	# Authenticate the host docker client to a GAR registry with bounded retry on
330	# the moby/moby#44350 premature-timeout transient (see
331	# RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE). docker login is the lone login/pull/push
332	# verb with no internal retry and a hardcoded 15s daemon->registry auth timeout;
333	# against a healthy-but-slow backend it fails where the endpoint is in fact fine.
334	# This mirrors the curl-transient tolerance in rbgu_http_json: retry only the
335	# surveyed signature, fail fast on everything else (real auth failures emit
336	# "unauthorized" and do not match), so clean-failure semantics are preserved.
337	# Stateless (no sentinel) — safe to call from any module regardless of kindle
338	# order, like rbgu_curl_status_is_transient_predicate.
339	#
340	# A second Palisade neighbor lives here for headless Cygwin: Docker Desktop's
341	# wincred helper cannot persist the credential auth just succeeded for
342	# (RBGC_DOCKER_WINCRED_HEADLESS_SIGNATURE), and Windows docker ignores an empty
343	# credsStore, so config alone cannot divert the store to the file store. On
344	# that exact signature the function bends ONCE: auth already succeeded, so it
345	# writes the credential straight into docker's base64 file store at
346	# ${HOME}/.docker/config.json (the `auths` map, the form WSL uses) and returns
347	# success. The caller's `docker push` reads that credential from the file, so
348	# the push completes without ever touching the Windows vault.
349	# Args: token registry_host
350	rbgo_docker_login() {
351	  local -r z_token="${1:?rbgo_docker_login: token required}"
352	  local -r z_host="${2:?rbgo_docker_login: registry host required}"
353	
354	  local z_attempt=0
355	  local z_stderr_file=""
356	  local z_rc=0
357	  local z_auth_b64=""
358	
359	  while :; do
360	    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
361	    z_stderr_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_docker_login_${z_attempt}_stderr.txt"
362	
363	    z_rc=0
364	    printf '%s' "${z_token}" \
365	      | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_host}" \
366	          > /dev/null 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
367	      || z_rc=$?
368	
369	    test "${z_rc}" -ne 0 || break
370	
371	    buc_log_pipe < "${z_stderr_file}"
372	
373	    # Palisade bend (docker's own credential store): under headless Cygwin the
374	    # wincred helper cannot persist the credential auth just succeeded for, and
375	    # an empty credsStore does not divert Windows docker to the file store
376	    # (verified — the CLI still detects wincred). Auth already succeeded, so
377	    # write the credential into the base64 file store ourselves — the on-disk
378	    # `auths` form WSL uses and `docker push` reads directly — and treat login
379	    # as done. credsStore is intentionally omitted so retrieval reads the file.
380	    # A real auth failure emits "unauthorized", never matches, and falls through
381	    # to the surveyed-transient / fail-fast handling below.
382	    if [[ "$(<"${z_stderr_file}")" == *"${RBGC_DOCKER_WINCRED_HEADLESS_SIGNATURE}"* ]]; then
383	      buc_warn "Docker wincred helper cannot persist headless (no interactive Windows logon); writing the authenticated credential to the base64 file store in \${HOME}/.docker/config.json. REMOVE this bend when the host gains a working credential vault."
384	      z_auth_b64=$(rbgo_base64_encode_string_capture "oauth2accesstoken:${z_token}") \
385	        || buc_die "Cannot base64-encode the docker credential for the file-store bend"
386	      mkdir -p "${HOME}/.docker" \
387	        || buc_die "Cannot create ${HOME}/.docker for the credential-store bend"
388	      printf '{"auths":{"%s":{"auth":"%s"}}}' "${z_host}" "${z_auth_b64}" > "${HOME}/.docker/config.json" \
389	        || buc_die "Cannot write ${HOME}/.docker/config.json credential-store bend"
390	      return 0
391	    fi
392	
393	    [[ "$(<"${z_stderr_file}")" == *"${RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE}"* ]] \
394	      || buc_die "Docker login to ${z_host} failed — see ${z_stderr_file}"
395	
396	    test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}" \
397	      || buc_die "Docker login to ${z_host} failed after ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} attempts (transient daemon->registry timeout, moby#44350) — see ${z_stderr_file}"
398	
399	    buc_warn "Docker login transient (attempt ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}, moby#44350 timeout) — retrying in ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}s"
400	    sleep "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}"
401	  done
402	}

58	# Convert milliseconds to a decimal seconds string suitable for sleep
59	zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture() {
60	  zrbgv_sentinel
61	
62	  local z_ms="${1}"
63	
64	  buc_log_args "Converting ${z_ms}ms to sleep duration"
65	
66	  # Compute whole seconds and remainder milliseconds using integer arithmetic
67	  local z_sec=$(( z_ms / 1000 ))
68	  local z_rem=$(( z_ms % 1000 ))
69	
70	  # Format as decimal with three fractional digits (sleep accepts e.g. 1.500)
71	  printf '%d.%03d' "${z_sec}" "${z_rem}"
72	}

100	# HTTP GET with bounded exponential-backoff retry on transient 5xx responses.
101	# On any non-5xx response (including auth errors), populates z_code_file and returns.
102	# On curl-network failure or exhausted 5xx retries, buc_die. The caller evaluates
103	# the final HTTP status via its own case-switch on the populated z_code_file.
104	zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry() {
105	  zrbgv_sentinel
106	
107	  local -r z_label="${1}"
108	  local -r z_url="${2}"
109	  local -r z_token="${3}"
110	  local -r z_resp_file="${4}"
111	  local -r z_code_file="${5}"
112	  local -r z_stderr_file="${6}"
113	
114	  local z_attempt=1
115	  local z_delay="${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}"
116	  local z_curl_status=0
117	  local z_code=""
118	
119	  while test "${z_attempt}" -le "${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"; do
120	    buc_log_args "${z_label}: HTTP GET attempt ${z_attempt}/${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"
121	
122	    z_curl_status=0
123	    rbuh_request "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}"          \
124	                      "${z_resp_file}" "${z_code_file}" "${z_stderr_file}" \
125	      || z_curl_status=$?
126	
127	    buc_log_args "${z_label}: curl exit status ${z_curl_status}"
128	    buc_log_pipe < "${z_stderr_file}"
129	
130	    test "${z_curl_status}" -eq 0 \
131	      || buc_die "${z_label}: curl failed (network/SSL/DNS) — see ${z_stderr_file}"
132	
133	    z_code=$(<"${z_code_file}") || buc_die "${z_label}: failed to read HTTP code file"
134	    test -n "${z_code}" || buc_die "${z_label}: empty HTTP code from curl"
135	
136	    case "${z_code}" in
137	      500|502|503|504)
138	        if test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"; then
139	          buc_step "${z_label}: transient HTTP ${z_code}, retrying in ${z_delay}s (attempt ${z_attempt}/${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS})"
140	          sleep "${z_delay}"
141	          z_delay=$(( z_delay * 2 ))
142	          z_attempt=$(( z_attempt + 1 ))
143	        else
144	          buc_die "${z_label}: repeated transient HTTP ${z_code} after ${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} attempts — see ${z_resp_file}"
145	        fi
146	        ;;
147	      *)
148	        return 0
149	        ;;
150	    esac
151	  done
152	}

154	# Extract '.error.message' from a JSON response file for diagnostic surfacing.
155	# Returns a fallback string for empty/missing/malformed bodies. jq stderr captured

157	zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture() {

167	    || { printf 'non-JSON response body — see %s\n' "${z_stderr_file}"; return 0; }
168	
169	  jq -r '.error.message // "no error.message field"' "${z_resp_file}" 2>>"${z_stderr_file}" \
170	    || printf 'jq extraction failed — see %s\n' "${z_stderr_file}"

282	######################################################################
283	# External Functions (rbgv_*)
284	
285	# Probe 1: JWT SA Access Probe
286	#
287	# Bounded propagation-absorbing retry. First HTTP 200/206 wins; HTTP 403 (the
288	# IAM-policy-read 'not yet' signature in the post-mint window) retries with delay;
289	# any other HTTP code dies with body summary. Past max attempts on 403, dies with
290	# body summary as 'budget exhausted'. Layers cleanly with rbgo_get_token_capture's
291	# JWT-signature retry: each layer recognizes its own 'not yet' and retries.
292	#
293	# All three roles have read access:
294	#   Governor  = Owner
295	#   Director  = repoAdmin
296	#   Retriever = reader
297	rbgv_jwt_sa_probe() {
298	  zrbgv_sentinel
299	
300	  local -r z_role="${1:-}"
301	  local -r z_count="${2:-1}"
302	  local -r z_delay_ms="${3:-0}"
303	
304	  buc_doc_brief "Probe JWT service account access for a given role against Artifact Registry"
305	  buc_doc_param "role"     "Role to probe: governor | director | retriever"
306	  buc_doc_param "count"    "Maximum attempts; first 2xx wins, retries on HTTP 403 (default: 1)"
307	  buc_doc_param "delay_ms" "Milliseconds to sleep between retries (default: 0)"
308	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
309	
310	  test -n "${z_role}"  || buc_die "role parameter required (governor | director | retriever)"
311	  test -n "${z_count}" || buc_die "count parameter required"
312	
313	  buc_step "JWT SA access probe: role=${z_role} count=${z_count} delay_ms=${z_delay_ms}"
314	
315	  local z_iter=1
316	  while test "${z_iter}" -le "${z_count}"; do
317	    buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}/${z_count}"
318	
319	    local z_code
320	    z_code=$(zrbgv_jwt_ar_probe_once_capture "${z_role}" "${z_iter}") \
321	      || buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: probe-once observation failed"
322	    test -n "${z_code}" || buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: empty HTTP code"
323	
324	    case "${z_code}" in
325	      200|206)
326	        buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: OK (HTTP ${z_code})"
327	        return 0
328	        ;;
329	      403)
330	        if test "${z_iter}" -lt "${z_count}"; then
331	          buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: 'not yet' (HTTP 403); retrying"
332	          if test "${z_delay_ms}" -gt 0; then
333	            local z_sleep
334	            z_sleep=$(zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture "${z_delay_ms}")
335	            buc_log_args "Sleeping ${z_sleep}s (${z_delay_ms}ms) before next attempt"
336	            sleep "${z_sleep}"
337	          fi
338	        else
339	          local z_err
340	          z_err=$(zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}" "${ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE}")
341	          buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: propagation budget exhausted (HTTP 403): ${z_err}"
342	        fi
343	        ;;
344	      *)
345	        local z_err
346	        z_err=$(zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}" "${ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE}")
347	        buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: unexpected HTTP ${z_code}: ${z_err}"
348	        ;;

350	
351	    z_iter=$(( z_iter + 1 ))
352	  done
353	
354	  buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}]: loop exited without verdict — internal logic error"
355	}

361	#   2. Call CRM projects.get on payor project to verify token works
362	#   3. Sleep for configured delay

364	  zrbgv_sentinel
365	
366	  local z_count="${1:-1}"
367	  local z_delay_ms="${2:-0}"
368	
369	  buc_doc_brief "Probe Payor OAuth access against Cloud Resource Manager (CRM) projects.get"
370	  buc_doc_param "count"    "Number of iterations (default: 1)"
371	  buc_doc_param "delay_ms" "Milliseconds to sleep between iterations (default: 0)"
372	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
373	
374	  test -n "${z_count}" || buc_die "count parameter required"
375	
376	  buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe: count=${z_count} delay_ms=${z_delay_ms}"
377	
378	  local z_iter=1
379	  while test "${z_iter}" -le "${z_count}"; do
380	    buc_step "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iter}/${z_count}"
381	
382	    zrbgv_payor_crm_probe_once "${z_iter}"
383	
384	    if test "${z_iter}" -lt "${z_count}" && test "${z_delay_ms}" -gt 0; then
385	      local z_sleep

387	      buc_log_args "Sleeping ${z_sleep}s (${z_delay_ms}ms) before next iteration"
388	      sleep "${z_sleep}"
389	    fi
390	
391	    z_iter=$(( z_iter + 1 ))
392	  done
